title: Adding multiple label items to a dropdown column from Zapier to Monday
excerpt: I’m trying add label items to a dropdown column on Monday from data thats being sent from Zapier. But the data that sent from Zapier gets combined into a single label item, I’m not sure how to separate my data so that th…

posts.author: rareskiils (RareSkills)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m trying add label items to a dropdown column on Monday from data thats being sent from Zapier. But the data that sent from Zapier gets combined into a single label item, I’m not sure how to separate my data so that they appear as separate label items. I’ve tried separating the data with commas, but that doesn’t seem to work.

posts.author: rehan.abdul (Rehan AQ | monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
You need to split by comma in order to add that. On make if let’s say string is a;b;c
then I will pass split(string, ,) then it will automatically create label and also check yes to create label if not exist. On zapier it is same as well.

posts.author: reyna789 (reyna kearns)
posts.cookedHtml: To add multiple label items to a Monday.com dropdown DMV NJ column from Zapier, use the Text step to split the combined labels string into individual labels. Then, use the Formatter step to join these labels with newline characters (\n). This creates the correct format for Monday.com, where each label appears on a separate line.
